rationale: The citing paper focuses on longitudinal changes in how resolvable vs. irresolvable attribute satisfaction drives product-quality satisfaction during ownership, while the cited paper focuses on experimental learning of brand–quality associations in portfolio branding contexts. The only linkage evidence provided is a single DOI-matched reference entry (no in-text citation context or reference text), so substantive use is not demonstrated, but a possible conceptual tie to quality-association formation means it is not safe to drop outright.
